coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether Lin’s recent late-night pace is a short-term spike or a sign of mis-scoped ownership at Head of Product level.
openingQuestion: What is still pulling your time most now that the launches are done, and which parts of that work genuinely need your direct ownership?
listenFor: whether they name specific meetings, decisions, or stakeholders that are still consuming evenings
if they distinguish one or two decisions only they should make from tasks they describe handing off vaguely
whether they give a concrete time frame like "just this week" versus speak about late nights as normal operating pace
avoid: do not praise the early launches so much that you normalize the three weeks of late nights
do not jump straight into solving resourcing or delegation before Lin has mapped what is actually driving the load
goodOutcome: You and Lin have agreed which Head of Product decisions stay with Lin over the next two weeks and one concrete area of ownership to push down to a team lead or PM.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example of work Lin personally carried after hours and use it to test whether it required Head of Product judgment or could have sat elsewhere.
